summary:
An elevated LDH while other biomarkers remain normal suggests malignancy; among possible diagnoses, a malignant teratoma best fits this profile as it uniquely elevates LDH levels. \boxed{Malignant Teratoma}

The summary captures the essential point about why LDH elevation indicates potential malignancy specifically pointing out that a malignant teratoma matches these criteria well compared to others. It stays concise focusing directly on what differentiates this case from more common scenarios involving ovarian masses. Let me know if you would like anything adjusted!
